edition: UL 48 — Standard for Electric Signs (14th Edition, current as of 2023)
scope: Electric signs and assemblies including illuminated channel letters, LED cabinet signs, neon signs, and electronic message centers
note: UL 48 is the primary product safety standard for electric signs manufactured under NAICS 339950. Compliance is required for NEC 600.3 listing requirement.
companion_standards: UL 879 — Electric Sign Components (LED drivers, ballasts)
UL 969 — Marking and Labeling Systems (adhesive labels/nameplates)
UL 8750 — Light Emitting Diode (LED) Equipment for Use in Lighting Products

sections.title: Scope
requirements: Covers electric signs that are complete assemblies including enclosures, wiring, and illumination sources.
Applies to signs rated 600 V or less.
Covers indoor, outdoor, and wet-location signs.
Does not cover signs using high-intensity discharge (HID) lamps unless specifically investigated.

checklist.item: Confirm sign is within UL 48 scope (rated ≤600 V, complete assembly).
If HID lamps used: confirm supplemental investigation with AHJ/NRTL.

sections.title: General Construction Requirements
requirements: §4.1: Sign enclosure (cabinet) must be constructed of materials suitable for the installation environment.
§4.2: Outdoor signs must use corrosion-resistant materials (aluminum, galvanized steel, or stainless steel for fasteners in coastal environments).
§4.3: All wiring within sign enclosure must be rated for the operating temperature and voltage.
§4.4: Minimum conductor size #18 AWG for internal sign wiring unless component is Listed for smaller gauge.
§4.5: Access panels and wiring compartments must be secured with tool-required fasteners (not accessible without tools).
§4.6: Internal wiring must be protected from sharp edges, heat sources, and moving parts.

checklist.item: Cabinet material is appropriate for environment (aluminum, galvanized, or 316 SS for coastal).
Internal wiring rated for operating temperature (min 60°C, 90°C preferred for LED cabinets).
Minimum #18 AWG internal conductors used throughout.
Access panels require a tool to open (screwdriver, hex key) — not hand-operable.
Internal wiring is secured and protected from cabinet edges (grommets, conduit where required).

sections.title: LED Sign Requirements
requirements: §5.1: LED drivers (power supplies) must be Listed under UL 879 or UL 879A for sign use.
§5.2: LED modules must be Listed under UL 879A or UL 8750.
§5.3: LED driver output voltage must not exceed the listed voltage of the LED modules.
§5.4: LED drivers must be rated for ambient temperature of installation (typically -40°C to +50°C for outdoor).
§5.5: LED module power density must not exceed the thermal management capacity of the sign enclosure.
§5.6: LED dimming controls must be Listed and compatible with the driver.
§5.7: Maximum LED module or driver case temperature during normal operation: 90°C on accessible surfaces.

checklist.item: All LED drivers are Listed under UL 879 or UL 879A — UL file number confirmed.
checklist.notes: Check UL Product iQ database at iq.ul.com for valid E-number.

checklist.item: LED modules are Listed under UL 879A or UL 8750 — UL file number confirmed.
LED driver output voltage ≤ rated input voltage of LED modules (no overvoltage).
LED driver ambient temperature rating covers installation range (outdoor: -40°C to +50°C).
checklist.notes: Verify driver datasheet for Ta rating.

checklist.item: Thermal management adequate — cabinet venting or heat sink prevents LED driver case exceeding 90°C.

sections.title: Neon Sign Requirements
requirements: §6.1: Neon transformers and electronic power supplies (EPS) for neon must be Listed under UL 2161.
§6.2: EPS must be rated at or above the actual tube load (mA and voltage).
§6.3: Secondary circuit wiring outside glass tubing must be GTO-15 cable (15 kV rated, flexible, high-temp insulation).
§6.4: Glass tubing must be supported at intervals not exceeding 4 ft (1.2 m) for tubes ≥ 15 mm diameter.
§6.5: Electrode connections must use crimped or compression-type connectors; soldered connections not permitted on secondary side.
§6.6: Maximum secondary open-circuit voltage from transformer: 15 kV rms.
§6.7: All metal parts of neon sign enclosure must be grounded; GTO-15 cable must not be subject to mechanical damage.

checklist.item: Neon transformer/EPS is Listed under UL 2161 — UL file number on transformer label.
EPS mA and voltage rating matches or exceeds tube load (don't underrate).
GTO-15 cable used for all secondary conductor runs outside glass tubing.
Neon tube supports at max 4-ft intervals for tubes ≥ 15 mm diameter.
Electrode connections are crimped or compression type (no soldered secondary connections).
Open-circuit secondary voltage does not exceed 15 kV rms.

sections.title: Enclosure and Environmental Protection
requirements: §7.1: Outdoor signs must have an ingress protection rating of at least IP54 (IEC 60529) or equivalent NEMA 3R.
§7.2: Wet-location signs must be rated IP55 or NEMA 4 minimum.
§7.3: Gaskets must be EPDM, neoprene, or silicone rated for outdoor UV and temperature cycling.
§7.4: Drain holes (weep holes) must be provided in outdoor enclosures to prevent water accumulation.
§7.5: Sign face (acrylic or polycarbonate) must be UV-stabilized for outdoor use.
§7.6: Electronic message center (EMC) LED matrices must have conformal coating on PCBs for outdoor/wet locations.

checklist.item: Outdoor sign enclosure rated min IP54 / NEMA 3R.
Wet-location sign enclosure rated min IP55 / NEMA 4.
Gasket material is EPDM, neoprene, or silicone (not foam weatherstripping).
Drain/weep holes present at bottom of outdoor enclosure.
Sign face (acrylic/polycarbonate) is UV-stabilized grade.
EMC/LED matrix PCBs have conformal coating for outdoor/wet use.
checklist.conditional: sign_type == EMC OR location_type in [outdoor, wet]

sections.title: Markings and Labels
requirements: §8.1: Nameplate must include: manufacturer name/trademark, voltage, current, frequency, number of signs.
§8.2: UL Listing mark must appear on sign exterior, adjacent to nameplate.
§8.3: Indoor/Outdoor/Wet Location classification must appear on nameplate or adjacent label.
§8.4: High-voltage warning labels required on access panels covering neon secondary conductors or terminals.
§8.5: Labels must be durable and legible throughout the sign's life — adhesive labels must meet UL 969.
§8.6: Maximum allowable conductor temperature markings required if wiring exceeds 60°C.

checklist.item: Nameplate affixed to sign exterior with all required data (manufacturer, V, A, Hz).
UL Listing mark (cUL, cULus, or ETL equivalent) visible on sign exterior.
Location use classification (Indoor/Outdoor/Wet) marked on sign.
HIGH VOLTAGE warning label on all access panels covering neon secondary wiring.
All adhesive labels meet UL 969 permanence requirements (UV, heat, moisture tested).

sections.title: Performance and Dielectric Tests
requirements: §9.1: Dielectric voltage-withstand test: 1000 V + 2× rated voltage applied for 1 minute between live parts and enclosure.
§9.2: Ground continuity: bonding resistance ≤ 0.1 Ω between any accessible metal part and ground terminal.
§9.3: Leakage current: ≤ 0.5 mA for portable signs; ≤ 3.5 mA for fixed signs.
§9.4: Temperature test: verify no component exceeds rated temperature after 1-hour steady-state operation.
§9.5: Rain test (outdoor signs): IP54 test — no water ingress after spray test per IEC 60529.

checklist.item: Dielectric hi-pot test passed at (1000 V + 2× rated voltage) for 1 min without breakdown.
checklist.notes: Performed by manufacturer at end-of-line; document test results.

checklist.item: Ground continuity: bonding resistance ≤ 0.1 Ω from enclosure to ground terminal.
Leakage current ≤ 3.5 mA (fixed sign) or ≤ 0.5 mA (portable sign).
Temperature test: no component exceeds rated temperature after 1-hour steady state.
Outdoor signs: IP54 rain test passed (IEC 60529 method).

install_notes: UL 48 listing is a factory-certification standard; field modifications may void listing.
Any field modification to a Listed sign requires re-evaluation by the original NRTL or AHJ field labeling.
Keep UL 48 listing documentation (UL file number, category code) on file for AHJ permit submissions.
UL Product iQ database (iq.ul.com) allows public verification of valid UL listings.
references: UL 48 Standard for Electric Signs, 14th Edition — UL Standards & Engagement
UL 879 / UL 879A — Electric Sign Components / LED-Based Sign Components
UL 2161 — Neon Sign Transformers and Electronic Power Supplies
UL 8750 — Light Emitting Diode (LED) Equipment for Lighting Products
IEC 60529 — Degrees of Protection Provided by Enclosures (IP Code)
